title: No-Trade Garbage Time Context
focus_questions: Is the market actually tradable right now, or only active-looking?
Are current signals producing outcome, or just conflicting noise?

summary.thesis: Garbage time is the environment where events, movement, and visible liquidity exist, but none of them provide enough edge to justify opening risk.
summary.operator_translation: Not every readable market is a tradable market; this card should suppress weak setup enthusiasm.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card to down-rank mediocre strategy candidates when context is conflicted, mid-auction, or noisy without result.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: active-looking market without clean edge. Required evidence: conflicting timeframe context, middle-of-auction location, or repeated events without durable outcome. Do not trade: just because the tape feels busy. Real trigger: none until a true boundary and real acceptance or rejection appear. Failure point: a genuine edge emerges at a meaningful location with outcome.

when_relevant: Recent event tags create attention but not durable result.
required_evidence: the market is trading away from a true edge or meaningful boundary
multiple events are appearing without real follow-through
cross-timeframe conflict or mid-auction churn is visible
confirmation_signals: multiple event tags appear but none produce durable acceptance or displacement
higher-timeframe context points one way while local execution logic points the other
price remains inside non-edge territory where both sides are still recycled
invalidation_signals: a real edge appears at a meaningful boundary
one side begins producing durable acceptance and displacement
cross-timeframe conflict resolves clearly in one direction
no_trade_conditions: Do not open in the middle of balance because recent candles feel active.
Do not open when lower-timeframe triggers conflict with stronger higher-timeframe location and no handoff is visible.
Do not mistake repeated event labels for real edge when none of them produce outcome.

entry_archetypes.name: suppression card
entry_archetypes.description: Use this card to suppress weak candidate promotion rather than to generate entries.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: If a true boundary and outcome emerge, hand off to a real setup card.

management_notes: This card should outrank mediocre setup cards when context quality is poor.
Its job is to stop unnecessary trades before the operator invents edge.
Once a real boundary and acceptance or rejection appear, this card should downgrade itself.
review_questions: Was the operator opening because the market was tradable or just because it was moving?
Did any observed event actually produce outcome, or only noise and attention churn?
Was there a real edge boundary nearby, or was the trade opened in auction middle?
